perturbation.original_text: "Intellectual Property Rights" or "IPR" means any and all intellectual property rights existing anywhere in the world associated with any and all (a) Patents, (b) Trademarks, (c) Copyrights, (d) Domain Names, (e) Software, (f) Trade Secrets and other confidential information, (g) all tangible embodiments of the foregoing in whatever form or medium and (h) any other legal protections and rights related to any of the foregoing. Intellectual Property Rights specifically excludes contractual rights (including license grants from third parties).
perturbation.changed_text: "Intellectual Property Rights" or "IPR" means any and all intellectual property rights existing anywhere in the world associated with any and all (a) Patents, (b) Trademarks, (c) Copyrights, (d) Domain Names, (e) Software, (f) Trade Secrets and other confidential information, (g) all tangible embodiments of the foregoing in whatever form or medium and (h) any other legal protections and rights related to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'Intellectual Property Rights specifically excludes contractual rights (including license grants from third parties),' we create an ambiguity in the definition of Intellectual Property Rights (IPR). Without this exclusion, it's unclear whether contractual rights like license grants are considered IPR. This omission creates an in-text contradiction with sections that deal with licenses, such as Section 3.01 and 4.01, as it's now uncertain whether these sections involve the granting of Intellectual Property Rights, as the definition has been altered to remove the original exclusion of contractual rights. It could be argued that granting a license grants IPR, which impacts obligations and liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Party or any member of its Group may assign or grant a license in or to any of its Intellectual Property Rights licensed to the other Party or any member of its Group pursuant to ARTICLE III or ARTICLE IV, unless such assignment or grant is subject to the licenses, covenants and restrictions set forth herein. For the avoidance of doubt, a non-exclusive license grant shall be deemed subject to the licenses granted herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excluded contractual rights (including license grants) from the definition of "Intellectual Property Rights." The changed text removes this exclusion, making it ambiguous whether license grants are considered IPR. This creates a direct conflict with the 'Contradicted Text', which restricts the assignment or licensing of Intellectual Property Rights. Because the definition of IPR is now unclear, it's uncertain whether the restrictions in the 'Contradicted Text' apply to license grants, creating a strong in-text contradiction that could impact enforceability and obligations related to licenses.

perturbation.original_text: All Trade Secrets and other confidential information of a Party disclosed to the other Party under this Agreement (including the Honeywell Content) shall be deemed confidential and proprietary information of the disclosing Party, shall be subject to the provisions of Section 7.09 of the Separation Agreement and may be used by the receiving Party for the express purpose of effecting the licenses granted herein.
perturbation.changed_text: All Trade Secrets and other confidential information of a Party disclosed to the other Party under this Agreement (including the Honeywell Content) shall be deemed confidential and proprietary information of the disclosing Party, and may be used by the receiving Party for the express purpose of effecting the licenses granted herein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'shall be subject to the provisions of Section 7.09 of the Separation Agreement', the confidentiality clause is altered. Section 7.09 of the Separation Agreement likely contains crucial details regarding the handling, duration, and permissible uses of confidential information. Without this reference, the receiving party's obligations regarding the disclosed Trade Secrets become unclear, especially when other clauses refer back to the 'confidentiality' obligations. This creates an internal contradiction because the contract continues to address matters relating to confidentiality, yet the core provisions governing it are no longer definitively linked.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) All SpinCo Trade Secrets shall be in or shall be moved to the physical possession of the SpinCo Group in a tangible form (including all copies thereof and all notes, extracts or summaries based thereon) prior to the Distribution Date. At the specific written request of SpinCo, Honeywell shall destroy or shall have destroyed any form or copy of any SpinCo Trade Secrets specified by SpinCo in such written request that are in the possession of Honeywell or any members of the Honeywell Group and were not used in the Honeywell Business as of immediately prior to the Distribution, other than SpinCo Trade Secrets that were electronically preserved or recorded by an electronic backup system prior to the Distribution Date and remain within a secure, encrypted data backup system that is subject to industry practice defense, protection and access restriction measures.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly subjects the handling of trade secrets to Section 7.09 of the Separation Agreement. By removing this reference in the changed text, the receiving party's obligations regarding the disclosed Trade Secrets become undefined. The contradicted text discusses the physical possession and destruction of SpinCo Trade Secrets by Honeywell, implying specific procedures and limitations tied to the confidentiality of those trade secrets. Because the changed text eliminates a specific tie to the separation agreement that may include conditions for how Honeywell must destroy copies of Trade Secrets, the contradicted text now lacks context, potentially leading to Honeywell destroying SpinCo Trade Secrets without following appropriate measures in the original agreement. This creates a contradiction as Honeywell now has a different set of obligations than if the change had not been made.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to Section 11.02 and Section 11.05, in the event of any actual or threatened default in, or breach of, any of the terms, conditions and provisions of this Agreement, the affected Party shall have the right to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief of its rights under this Agreement, in addition to any and all other rights and remedies at Law or in equity, and all such rights and remedies shall be cumulative. The other Party shall not oppose the granting of such relief on the basis that money damages are an adequate remedy. The Parties agree that the remedies at Law for any breach or threatened breach hereof, including monetary damages, are inadequate compensation for any loss and that any defense in any action for specific performance that a remedy at Law would be adequate is waived. Any requirements for the securing or posting of any bond with such remedy are waived.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of any actual or threatened default in, or breach of, any of the terms, conditions and provisions of this Agreement, the affected Party shall have the right to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief of its rights under this Agreement, in addition to any and all other rights and remedies at Law or in equity, and all such rights and remedies shall be cumulative. The other Party shall not oppose the granting of such relief on the basis that money damages are an adequate remedy. The Parties agree that the remedies at Law for any breach or threatened breach hereof, including monetary damages, are inadequate compensation for any loss and that any defense in any action for specific performance that a remedy at Law would be adequate is waived. Any requirements for the securing or posting of any bond with such remedy are waived.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'Subject to Section 11.02 and Section 11.05' at the beginning of Section 11.06, the availability of specific performance is made unconditional. Sections 11.02 and 11.05 outline dispute resolution and interim relief processes, respectively. Removing the dependency on these sections creates an internal contradiction: If there is a dispute on any of the terms outlined in the contract, the specific performance right is immediate, bypassing the dispute resolution process. If the sections were followed, interim measures may have provided a means of specific performance, therefore it isn't required here and is therefore contradictory in nature.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that either Party, acting reasonably, forms the view that another Party has caused a material breach of the terms of this Agreement, then the Party that forms such a view shall serve written notice of the alleged breach on the other Parties and the Parties shall work together in good faith to resolve any such alleged breach within 12 Source: GARRETT MOTION INC., 8-K, 10/1/2018 Table of Contents thirty (30) days of such notice (a "Dispute"). If any such alleged breach is not so resolved, then a senior executive of each Party shall, in good faith, attempt to resolve any such alleged breach within the following thirty (30) days of the referral of the matter to the senior executives. If no resolution is reached with respect to any such alleged breach in accordance with the procedures contained in this Section 11.02, then the Parties may seek to resolve such matter in accordance with Section 11.03, Section 11.04 and Section 11.06
perturbation.justification: The original text made the right to specific performance 'subject to Section 11.02 and Section 11.05,' which outline dispute resolution. By removing this phrase, the changed text allows a party to seek specific performance immediately upon an alleged breach, bypassing the dispute resolution process detailed in Section 11.02. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which mandates a good-faith effort to resolve disputes before seeking other remedies, including those in Section 11.06. The change removes a condition precedent to seeking specific performance, thereby creating a conflict.
